steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The notes describe a research study on lizard clutch size. The student wants a sentence that emphasizes the aim of the research — what the researchers set out to investigate — not their methods or results.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Researchers wanted to know which factors influence lizard egg clutch size because such factors have been well studied in birds but not in lizards.: Directly states the research aim (what they wanted to know) and the motivating reason (well studied in birds, not lizards). ✓
  • Choice B — After they obtained data for over 3,900 lizard species, researchers determined that larger clutch size was associated with environments in higher latitudes that have more seasonal change.: Emphasizes a finding at the end of the study, not the aim before it began. ✗
  • Choice C — We now know that lizards in higher-latitude environments may lay larger clutches to take advantage of shorter windows of favorable conditions.: Emphasizes a conclusion or interpretation, not the research aim. ✗
  • Choice D — Researchers obtained clutch-size and habitat data for over 3,900 lizard species and analyzed the data with statistical models.: Emphasizes the methodology of the study, not its aim. ✗
A is correct.
